hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve complementarity with SSTR2 ECL3, increasing selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid but must be refined to prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance SSTR2 selectivity. The focus should shift from optimizing ddG alone to optimizing the Δmargin metric.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding in SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket interactions while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or hydrogen-bonding interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining compatibility with SSTR2 binding.
